text: The Nature Faction is all about Healing and supporting your island.
The Tinker Faction is all about quick triggers, but can easily backfire.
The Magic Faction is all about buffs and spreading stats around.
The Novus Faction is all about innovation and flexibility, it'll scale on any island.
The Ancient Faction is all about destruction and sacrifice, but very powerful.
The Town Role is all about scaling up and expanding the strength of your island.
The Production Role is all about value, get them as early as possible.
The Solo Type is for units that have powerful trigger effects, they can never target each other.
Wins are more important than rounds for your Rating. The higher you climb, the less you'll get!
Units can only have their speed reduced by half. A unit with a trigger speed of 2 seconds can't go below 1 second.
Finding multiple copies of the same unit gets harder and harder, especially on higher tiers.
Holding spacebar shows some extra useful information, hovering over the UI will highlight those units.
Hovering a weapon will highlight its target, you can also hover units in the combat log.
Start of combat triggers from left to right, then up to down.
You can move units from your island to your inventory by right clicking them.
You can't win every round or survive every run. Good players make the most of a bad run.
Don't like your encounters? Selecting a 1-cost encounter is a cheap way to reroll while still developing your island.
Adjacent units include those on the corners. Hover over any unit to see which tiles it affects.
Explosive damage is dealt as a percentage of the original damage to all units adjacent to the primary target.
Pierce damage is dealt as a percentage of the original damage to a single unit directly behind the primary target.
Stuns all have a duration, which you can always find in the unit details panel.
After losing a battle, you take 1 point of damage for every tile that's occupied by an enemy unit. In other words, bigger units hurt!
The Fatigue Wizard appears after 40 seconds in order to end fights that have dragged on for too long.
Lifesteal effects can add to a unit's max health, not just restore any missing health.
Buying certain units will unlock follow-up units in future encounters.
Encounters have different rarities based on the number of unit options they offer. Epic encounters glow purple and offer a choice of 6.
Enchantments can be removed from units and placed on a new unit at any time. Just click them to add them back into your inventory.
Units fit into four different tag categories: Tier, Faction, Role, and Type.
Weapons have three different projectile speeds, Slow, Medium, and Fast. Slower weapons might miss their target if it's destroyed before the projectile arrives!
You can find a bunch of useful information (including these tips) in the Library.
When a unit is resurrected, its 'Start of Combat' effect activates again.
Every time you buy a Weapon, it becomes less likely you'll find another. Supporting the Weapons you have is a better path to success.
Right-clicking an encounter will open up the library with active filters for that shop.
You can move the inventory by dragging it around. To go back, click the reset button in the top left.
